svg.text: Multus Thick mode Internals: shim → multus.sock UDS RPC → daemon → chroot+exec delegate (host fs view)
Why containerized daemon needs chroot to reach host /opt/cni/bin and host /etc/cni/net.d so each delegate sees the same fs as if invoked from host
Section 1 — Process layout (multus-shim in kubelet's CNI runtime context vs multus-daemon DaemonSet Pod with hostPath mounts)
Section 2 — UDS RPC (HTTP-over-UDS protocol; JSON body carries CNI args; daemon dispatches to handler)
dummy (URL host is fake; UDS overrides)
cache hit on Pod + NAD informer → no apiserver call
Section 3 — chroot+exec mechanism (Go SysProcAttr.Chroot at fork; daemon stays containerized; child sees host fs view)
Chroot is a SysProcAttr field; child does chroot before exec
→ resolves on the new fs root
filesystem view: before chroot vs after chroot
daemon refers to host bin as
but the binary expects to see
SAME paths a CNI plugin invoked
from the host kubelet would see;
a host fs — they keep working.
Daemon stays containerized so k8s manages its lifecycle (DaemonSet, restartPolicy, image upgrade, log collection)

but each delegate must see the host fs — that's where /opt/cni/bin/* and /etc/cni/net.d/ live, and IPAM state files like /var/lib/cni/networks/. Chroot bridges the two worlds without unmounting the daemon's own rootfs.
Source: multus-cni v4.2.4 pkg/server/exec_chroot.go (ChrootExec.ExecPlugin), deployments/multus-daemonset-thick.yml, pkg/server/api/{api.go, shim.go, socket.go}
